title: tephrosia flagellaris
intro: Tephrosia flagellaris, also known as the "flagellate bean," is a perennial plant species in the legume family that is native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tephrosia flagellaris include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
